evidence_sentence: the Rht-B1b and Rht-D1b alleles produce only about a 20% reduction in height
notes: Rht-B1b = GAIN-OF-FUNCTION stabilised DELLA (Rht-B1 homeolog B); encoded gm=2.0

evidence_sentence: the Rht-B1b and Rht-D1b alleles produce only about a 20% reduction in height
notes: Rht-D1b = GAIN-OF-FUNCTION stabilised DELLA (Rht-B1 homeolog D); encoded gm=2.0

evidence_sentence: Rht-B1c and Rht-D1c are about half the size of the appropriate tall controls
notes: Rht-B1c = severe stabilised-DELLA allele (stronger than Rht-B1b); encoded gm=2.5

evidence_sentence: the extreme dwarfism of Rht-D1c is due to overexpression of the semidwarfing Rht-D1b allele, caused by an increase in gene copy number
notes: Rht-D1c = severe stabilised-DELLA allele on D genome; encoded gm=2.5

evidence_sentence: Plants carrying the Rht-B1d mutation are less severely dwarfed than those carrying Rht-B1b alone
notes: Rht-B1d = mild stabilised-DELLA allele; encoded gm=1.5 (slight)

evidence_sentence: it is likely that the Rht-B1e mutation confers its GA-insensitive semidwarf phenotype in a manner analogous to Rht-B1b
notes: Rht-B1e = moderate stabilised-DELLA allele; encoded gm=2.0

evidence_sentence: The Rht1+2 combination produced reductions up to 64% compared to the tall variety
notes: Double Rht-B1b + Rht-D1b; both DELLA homeologs stabilised, additive dwarfing

evidence_sentence: wild-type alleles designated as Rht-A1a, Rht-B1a, and Rht-D1a
notes: Rht-B1a WT allele control — no modifications

evidence_sentence: Rht-D1a (tall)
notes: Rht-D1a WT allele control — no modifications

evidence_sentence: Rht-B1c.22 (tall): stem length 15-22% longer than Rht-B1b
notes: Rht-B1c overgrowth suppressor (ovg): loss-of-function of the Rht-B1c allele restores WT-like DELLA; baseline=Rht-B1c alone (gm=2.5), perturbed=RHT_B1 restored to 1.0

evidence_sentence: combined platz1 mutant showed a significant reduction in plant height (12.7%), peduncle length (18.7%)
notes: PLATZ-A1 knockout → direct mapping

evidence_sentence: UBI::PLATZ1 overexpression: Taller plants; longer peduncles; male sterile
notes: PLATZ-A1 overexpression → direct mapping

evidence_sentence: ga3ox2 triple mutant: GA1 levels ~3-fold lower than wild-type in seedlings; severely dwarfed, infertile
notes: TaGA3ox2 triple homeolog KO (severe biosynthesis loss); TAGA3OX is the composite family node

evidence_sentence: ga3ox3 Mutants: 3.0-4.5 cm shorter than wild-type; grain width/TGW reduced
notes: TaGA3ox3 triple KO = slight dwarf; paralog with milder contribution to bioactive-GA pool; encoded as KD on composite TAGA3OX

evidence_sentence: ga1ox1 Mutant: Bioactive GA1/GA4 extremely high; 10.7 cm taller in 2021 field trial
notes: TaGA1ox1-B1 KO → loss of GA-inactivating oxidase → more bioactive GA → taller; matches TAGA1OX1 → (-1) Gibberellin edge

evidence_sentence: twice-weekly sprays of 10 μM GA3 almost completely restored the height and fertility
notes: TaGA3ox2 triple KO + exogenous GA3: biosynthesis rescue. Convention: baseline=mutant alone (TAGA3OX gm=0.0); expected=increased vs mutant (raw expected=unchanged was reported vs WT)

evidence_sentence: Wild-type seedlings increased 30% with GA3 treatment
notes: WT + GA3 treatment → Gibberellin exogenous_supply=1.0

evidence_sentence: Rht-B1c showed only 5% non-significant increase, confirming complete GA insensitivity
notes: TRAP-3 test: Rht-B1c + GA3. Stabilised DELLA cannot be degraded even at high GA; baseline=mutant (Rht-B1c alone); expected=unchanged vs mutant

evidence_sentence: Rht18, a dominant and gibberellin (GA) responsive mutant, is genetically and functionally distinct from the widely used GA-insensitive semidwarfing genes
notes: Rht18 = GA-responsive dwarf via elevated TaGA2ox-A9; GOF encoded as gm=2.0

evidence_sentence: Rht24b reduced plant height, but had no yield penalty; it significantly increased nitrogen use efficiency, photosynthetic rate and the expression of related genes
notes: Rht24b = GOF allele of Rht24 (elevated TaGA2ox-A9); gm=2.0

evidence_sentence: Rht12 dwarf plants screened for tall suppressors; suppressors carry loss-of-function mutations in GA2oxidaseA13
notes: Rht12 = GOF elevating TaGA2ox-A13 → more GA catabolism → dwarf; gm=2.0

evidence_sentence: Rht12, Rht18, and Rht14 share the same height-reducing mechanism
notes: Rht14 is a GA-responsive dwarf on 6A via elevated TaGA2ox; not a separate network node. Closest proxy in network is Rht18 (same mechanism family)

evidence_sentence: tall overgrowth individuals contain loss-of-function mutations in GA2oxidaseA13
notes: Rht12 overgrowth revertant: LOF mutation in TaGA2ox-A13 within an Rht12 (GOF) background. baseline=Rht12 OE parent (gm=2.0); perturbed=RHT12 reverted to gm=0.0; expected=increased vs parent

evidence_sentence: The Reduced height 8 (Rht8) semi-dwarfing gene reduces stature of wheat and improves lodging resistance, without compromising grain yield
notes: Rht8c = hypomorphic TaGRP2 allele causing modest GA reduction; encoded as gm=0.5

evidence_sentence: Rht-B13b plants exhibited approximately 17-34% height reduction primarily through shortened peduncle development
notes: Rht13 GOF = elevated PR gene expression / NLR-like dwarfism; gm=2.0

evidence_sentence: Triple mutants (with premature stop codons in all three TaBRI1 homoeologues) produced severe dwarfism, malformed leaves and sterility
notes: TaBRI1 triple homeolog KO → severe BR-insensitive dwarf; gm=0.0

evidence_sentence: Missense mutants in TaBRI1-A1 proved most promising: the less severe variant was 35% shorter than the wild type
notes: TaBRI1-A1 missense (single homeolog, partial LOF); gm=0.5

evidence_sentence: znf-bb (single): Shorter (8.4 cm); Slight reduction; no pleiotropy
notes: TaZnF-B LOF → derepressed TABKI1 → impaired BR signalling; gm=0.0

evidence_sentence: znf-bb rht1-bb (double): Similar to WT; Longer spikes, larger grains
notes: Rht-B1b + TaZnF-B double mutant; paper reports phenotype similar to WT (epistatic suppression); expected=unchanged vs WT

evidence_sentence: The tad11-2a-1 mutant displayed dwarfism, smaller grains, sensitivity to 24-epiBL, and reduced endogenous BR contents
notes: TaD11-2A single-homeolog KO with detectable moderate dwarfing; encoded gm=0.5 (partial) rather than composite gm=0.997 because phenotype is significant

evidence_sentence: the plant height of these mutants was reduced by 3.7-5 cm compared with that of Fielder
notes: VRN1 single-homeolog KO in Fielder - Liu 2025 reports 3.7-5 cm height reduction (~4%). Composite gm loosened from 0.997 convention to 0.9 to reflect paper-specific dosage sensitivity while preserving 'slight decrease' magnitude class.

evidence_sentence: VRN1-5A/5B/5D redundantly regulate heading date and plant height in wheat
notes: VRN1 triple homeolog KO → full loss → strong dwarf

evidence_sentence: increased GRF4 abundance boosts grain yield and NUE of rice and wheat GRVs without increasing mature plant height
notes: TaGRF4 OE; Li et al. 2018 explicitly state GRF4 OE increases yield 'without increasing mature plant height'. TAGRF4 removed from network in refinement iteration 1 because its DELLA interaction is post-translational competition (protein binding), not transcriptional regulation of DELLA levels - an algebraic-framework mismatch. Test now untestable in this network.

evidence_sentence: Rht3 | 4BS | GA-insensitive (DELLA) | ~64% reduction
notes: Rht3 = alias for the Rht-B1c severe-dwarf allele; encoded gm=2.5

evidence_sentence: Rht4 | 2BL | GA-responsive | ~12% reduction
notes: Rht4 = alias for a Rht-D1 semidwarf allele; encoded gm=2.0

evidence_sentence: Rht5 | 3BS | GA-responsive (P450) | ~50% reduction
notes: Rht5 is a distinct GA-insensitive dwarf locus on 3BS (not Rht-B1/D1). The underlying gene is not resolved in the current network; flagged as not_in_network

evidence_sentence: Expression of these N-terminal truncated proteins in transgenic lines demonstrated their ability to cause strong dwarfism
notes: Transgenic OE of Rht-B1b (GOF stabilised DELLA) → severe dwarfing

evidence_sentence: Wheat seedlings treated with 10 μM α-naphthaleneacetic acid (synthetic auxin) showed differential upregulation and downregulation of specific TaARF members within 0.5-3 hours post-treatment
notes: NAA (synthetic auxin) treatment → Auxin exogenous_supply=1.0

evidence_sentence: TaARF4-B was strongly associated with PH and root depth at the tillering, jointing and grain fill stages
notes: TaARF4-B haplotype = partial LOF of TaARF4 B homeolog; encoded gm=0.5

evidence_sentence: GA treatment failed to promote coleoptile elongation in Rht-B1b-containing lines, confirming the proteins function independently of GA-induced degradation pathways
notes: TRAP-3 test: Rht-B1b + GA3. Stabilised DELLA cannot be degraded even at high GA; baseline=mutant (Rht-B1b alone); expected=unchanged vs mutant. Raw expected=decreased was reported vs WT.

evidence_sentence: GA biosynthetic genes are essential for normal height; chemical inhibition with paclobutrazol (GA biosynthesis inhibitor) phenocopies ga3ox2 dwarf
notes: Paclobutrazol: GA-biosynthesis inhibitor (targets ent-kaurene oxidase, functionally reducing GA20ox output). Mapped to TAGA20OX gm=0.1

evidence_sentence: reduced BR biosynthesis resulted in smaller grains and stunted growth
notes: Brassinazole: inhibitor of DWF4 (BR biosynthesis). Mapped to TADWF4 gm=0.1

evidence_sentence: 24-epiBL treatment on WT wheat promotes BR signaling and elongation
notes: Brassinolide (BL) treatment → Brassinosteroid exogenous_supply=1.0

evidence_sentence: Triple mutants produced severe dwarfism; BR application unable to rescue BR-insensitive phenotype
notes: Trap-5 analog: TaBRI1 triple KO + BL. BR receptor missing → exogenous BL cannot be perceived → unchanged vs mutant. Raw expected=decreased was reported vs WT.

evidence_sentence: Overexpression of TaDWF4-B in cv. Fielder led to significantly increased growth compared to the WT even under low N conditions
notes: TaDWF4 overexpression → more BR biosynthesis → taller

evidence_sentence: Overexpression of TaPIF4 produced significantly greater plant height and longer spike length
notes: TaERF-A1 OE → activates PIF → taller

evidence_sentence: TaERF-A1JD6 protein accumulates at lower levels; Ser178-Phe (S178F) substitution influences the stability; considerably less effective at activating TaPIF4 expression, producing semi-dwarfism
notes: TaERF-A1 JD6 LOF allele → less PIF activation → shorter

evidence_sentence: Overexpression of TaPIF4 produced significantly greater plant height and longer spike length
notes: TaPIF4 OE mapped to PIF composite node (family represents TaPIF class)

evidence_sentence: TaPIF4 knockout reduced these traits
notes: TaPIF4 KO mapped to PIF composite node

evidence_sentence: the average PHs of the wild-type (WT), RNAi lines 1 (Ri-1) and 2 (Ri-2) were 94.6, 83.6 and 79.2 cm, respectively
notes: TaOSCA1.4 RNAi → Ca2+ channel knockdown; gm=0.1

evidence_sentence: Ppd-D1a showed a significant reduction effect (9.7 cm, 10.2%) on plant height, though much weaker than Rht12
notes: Ppd-D1a = photoperiod-insensitive GOF allele (broader expression → stronger output); PPD_D1 → (-1) Plant_Height, so GOF → slight decrease

evidence_sentence: The dwarfing allele Rht12b reduced plant height by 5.4-8.2 cm
notes: Rht12b = allelic variant of Rht12 (GOF elevating TaGA2ox-A13); gm=2.0

evidence_sentence: nitrate reduces the abundance of DELLAs by increasing GA contents through activation of GA metabolism gene expression
notes: High nitrate treatment → Nitrogen exogenous_supply=1.0 (nitrogen is an ENVIRONMENT source node)

evidence_sentence: the growth restraint conferred by nitrate deficiency is partially rescued in global-DELLA mutant
notes: Low-nitrogen stress encoded as reduced Nitrogen source level (gm=0.1)
